key_points: A polynomial is an algebraic expression made up of variables, coefficients and non-negative integer powers
Terms in a polynomial are separated by addition or subtraction
The degree of a polynomial is determined by the highest exponent of the variable
Polynomials can be classified as constant, linear, quadratic and cubic
A linear polynomial has exactly one zero
A quadratic polynomial can have at most two zeroes
Factorisation expresses a polynomial as a product of simpler polynomials
Algebraic identities help simplify polynomial expressions
examples: Zero example: For x − 7, zero is 7
exam_tips: Always check the highest power to determine the degree of a polynomial
Identify the number of terms before classifying the polynomial
Use algebraic identities to simplify expressions faster
Practice factorisation techniques for quadratic expressions
Substitute values carefully when evaluating polynomials
